concerns.text: FR-005 anchors to US-1, but the requirement text contains '[deferred]' placeholders for the interval types ('[deferred] t-intervals and [deferred] z-intervals'). While the anchor exists, the requirement itself is incomplete and ambiguous regarding the specific intervals to compute, which contradicts the explicit 't-intervals and z-intervals' defined in US-1 and the research question. The '[deferred]' marker is misused here as it refers to the *type* of interval, not an empirical value.

FR-008 anchors to US-3, but the requirement text uses '[deferred]' for the third confidence level in the set '{90%, 95%, [deferred]}'. US-3 explicitly defines the set as '(90%, 95%, 99%)'. The requirement should reflect the concrete set defined in the story to avoid ambiguity, as '[deferred]' is intended for empirical values, not defined parameter sets.

SC-004 anchors to US-3, but the success criteria text uses '[deferred]' placeholders for the confidence levels in the set '{[deferred], [deferred], [deferred]}'. US-3 explicitly defines the set as '(90%, 95%, 99%)'. The success criteria should list the specific levels to be measured against, as the set is a defined scope, not an empirical value to be deferred.
